Condition-Based Maintenance at Scale

Part 1: Planning Your Program

Home Industries Proof Resources Contact Assets

The Strategic Plan

Transitioning to condition-based maintenance requires careful strategic planning. This is the first of four articles covering the journey from pilot program to enterprise-wide implementation.

Why Condition-Based Maintenance?

Traditional time-based maintenance is inefficient. Condition-based maintenance optimizes schedules based on actual asset health, reducing costs and improving reliability.

Planning Phase

Your planning should address:

  • Asset criticality assessment
  • ROI analysis and business case development
  • Technology selection and vendor evaluation
  • Scope definition for pilot and scale phases
  • Timeline and milestone planning
  • Budget allocation across implementation phases

Next: People

In Part 2, we'll explore the organizational and cultural factors that determine success in CBM transformation.

Continue to Part 2: People
